Title: Cytokinin is required for escape but not release from auxin mediated apical dominance

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; early CK biosynthetic step and decapitation-responsive member
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an isopentenyl group to adenine nucleotides, generating cytokinin nucleotide precursors for iP/tZ-type cytokinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote bud activation and branching, especially allowing escape from auxin-mediated apical dominance under favorable conditions such as high nitrate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem and whole-plant cytokinin supply influencing axillary buds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and vegetative shoot tissues; stem transcript strongly induced (~8-fold) 6 h after decapitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stem of two-node segments after decapitation; contributes to vegetative branching
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of IPT3 reduces branching in intact plants, but IPT3 induction is not required for bud release after decapitation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after decapitation; single and higher-order loss-of-function mutants; decapitation branching assay; grafting; nodal auxin-response assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT3 transcript was the only tested IPT strongly induced by decapitation in stem tissue. 2. ipt3 mutants showed reduced branching in intact plants. 3. Despite decapitation responsiveness, IPT3 was not required for normal bud release after decapitation unless combined with other IPT mutations causing bud-initiation defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order IPT loss causes severe branching defects, stunting in quadruple mutant, and compromised bud initiation/empty axils.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance branching plasticity under high nitrate, but manipulation should consider redundancy with IPT5/IPT7 and risk of developmental defects from excessive CK perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Decapitation; high versus low nitrate; apical auxin (NAA)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray in buds; GEO GSE59741
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Root- or shoot-derived wild-type CK supply could rescue branching in ipt3,5,7 grafts, implying systemic CK contribution. Reduced decapitation branching in higher-order mutants likely reflects bud initiation defects rather than altered auxin sensitivity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an isopentenyl group to adenine nucleotides during early cytokinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and vegetative shoot tissues; not significantly induced by decapitation in tested stem assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutant reduces branching in intact plants; quadruple loss including IPT1 abolishes branching.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after decapitation; loss-of-function mutant branch counting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT1 contributes redundantly to CK biosynthesis supporting intact branching. 2. IPT1 transcript was not significantly altered by decapitation in stems. 3. Inclusion of ipt1 in the quadruple mutant exacerbated branching defects to nearly branchless plants.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with other IPTs causes severe stunting and absence of branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful only in combination-aware engineering of cytokinin biosynthesis; avoid strong loss-of-function because of major architectural penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray in buds; GEO GSE59741
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as a relevant CK biosynthetic family member, but not the main decapitation-responsive IPT in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an isopentenyl group to adenine nucleotides during early cytokinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and vegetative shoot tissues; not significantly induced by decapitation in tested stem assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Vegetative tissues contributing to branching and nitrate-responsive branching plasticity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutant reduces branching in intact plants; acts redundantly with IPT3 and IPT7, especially under high nitrate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after decapitation; single and higher-order mutant branch counts; nitrate branching assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT5 contributes to CK-dependent branching in intact plants. 2. IPT5 was not significantly induced by decapitation in stems. 3. Combined loss with IPT3 and IPT7 impaired branching plasticity under high nitrate.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order loss contributes to bud initiation defects and reduced branch number.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning nitrate-responsive branching via CK biosynthesis, preferably with moderate or tissue-specific modification.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray in buds; GEO GSE59741
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single ipt5 behaved like wild type under nitrate assay, indicating redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an isopentenyl group to adenine nucleotides during early cytokinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and vegetative shoot tissues; not significantly induced by decapitation in tested stem assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Vegetative tissues contributing to branching and nitrate-responsive branching plasticity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutant reduces branching in intact plants; functions redundantly with IPT3 and IPT5 in high-nitrate branching response.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after decapitation; single and higher-order mutant branch counts; grafting; nitrate branching assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPT7 contributes to CK-dependent branching in intact plants. 2. IPT7 transcript was not significantly induced by decapitation in stems. 3. Loss with IPT3 and IPT5 reduced branching and high-nitrate responsiveness.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order loss contributes to empty axils and reduced bud initiation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering CK-dependent branching plasticity, but redundancy and bud-initiation defects must be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray in buds; GEO GSE59741
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Wild-type root or shoot can rescue ipt3,5,7 graft branching, indicating systemic CK supply can compensate.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; cytokinin signaling component with receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cytokinin signaling controlling bud activation in the presence of apical auxin
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement for cytokinin-mediated bud activation despite canonical role as CK signaling feedback inhibitor
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Member of decisive type-A ARR clade required for CK to overcome auxin-mediated bud inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mutant hextuple retained normal CK-induced transcription of marker genes, indicating ARR3 clade is not required for tested transcriptional responses but is required for bud outgrowth response
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cytokinin; nitrate-associated CK increase; apical auxin context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Typically part of CK feedback signaling; paper suggests auxin-CK feedback loop at system level
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Member of a six-gene clade selected as CK-responsive in buds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in buds supplied with basal CK plus apical auxin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss within the arr3,4,5,6,7,15 clade reduces intact branching and impairs CK-mediated bud activation, but does not impair decapitation response.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bud microarray; qRT-PCR confirmation; hextuple loss-of-function mutant; isolated one-node bud assays; nitrate branching assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR3 is part of a CK-responsive type-A ARR clade induced in buds by CK. 2. The clade is required for CK-mediated bud activation in presence of apical auxin. 3. Loss of the clade reduces branching in intact plants but not bud release after decapitation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced branching under high nitrate and in intact plants when clade is disrupted.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for modulating branching plasticity, but broad CK signaling roles and family redundancy/pleiotropy warrant caution.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Basal BA; apical NAA; high versus low nitrate; decapitation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed only as part of the hextuple mutant, not singly.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement for CK-mediated bud activation in context of type-A ARR clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the decisive CK-responsive ARR clade required for CK escape from apical dominance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not required for tested CK-induced marker transcript accumulation in buds
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cytokinin; nitrate-associated CK increase; apical auxin context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CK-responsive member of selected type-A ARR clade
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in buds by CK plus auxin relative to auxin alone
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes within the hextuple mutant to reduced intact branching and CK-resistant bud activation phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qRT-PCR; hextuple mutant branching and nodal assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR4 was among the most strongly CK-induced transcripts in buds. 2. Together with ARR3/5/6/7/15 it is required for CK-mediated bud outgrowth. 3. It is not required for decapitation-induced bud release when considered in the hextuple mutant.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Clade disruption lowers branching under favorable conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for branching control, but alteration may have complex CK signaling consequences.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Basal BA; apical NAA; high nitrate

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement within type-A ARR clade for CK-mediated bud activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the decisive clade enabling escape from apical dominance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: CK marker induction retained in hextuple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in buds by CK in presence of apical auxin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required within the hextuple mutant for normal CK-mediated bud activation and intact branching.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qRT-PCR; hextuple mutant branching and bud assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR5 is strongly induced by CK in auxin-inhibited buds. 2. The ARR clade containing ARR5 is necessary for CK to promote bud outgrowth. 3. The clade is dispensable for release after decapitation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced branch plasticity when clade is mutated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for tuning architecture under nutrient-rich conditions, but family-wide effects complicate deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Basal BA; apical NAA; decapitation; nitrate supply

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement within the ARR clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the clade essential for CK-mediated escape from apical dominance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not needed for tested CK-induced transcriptional responses in buds
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cytokinin; nitrate status; apical auxin context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CK-responsive type-A ARR identified in bud microarray
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in buds by CK plus auxin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes within arr3,4,5,6,7,15 to reduced intact branching and impaired CK response of buds.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR6 is one of the CK-induced bud markers. 2. The ARR clade containing ARR6 is necessary for CK-induced bud activation. 3. Decapitation response remains normal without the clade.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad CK signaling perturbation may alter architecture plasticity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential architecture regulator but likely pleiotropic.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement within type-A ARR clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the decisive CK-responsive regulator set
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: CK-responsive marker expression remains inducible without clade
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cytokinin; nitrate-associated systemic CK; auxin context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among top bud transcripts induced by CK plus auxin versus auxin alone
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of hextuple mutant showing reduced branching and defective CK-induced bud activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qRT-PCR; hextuple mutant branching and bud assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR7 is strongly induced by CK in inhibited buds. 2. ARR7-containing clade is required for CK to overcome auxin-mediated bud inhibition. 3. The clade is dispensable for decapitation-induced release.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces branching under intact/high-nitrate conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May contribute to nutrient-responsive branching engineering, but family-level redundancy and pleiotropy are concerns.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-A Arabidopsis response regulator; receiver domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive requirement within type-A ARR clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the key CK-responsive ARR clade required for escape from apical dominance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not required for induction of tested CK-responsive transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in buds by CK plus auxin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes within the hextuple mutant to reduced intact branching and loss of CK-mediated bud activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray; qRT-PCR; hextuple mutant branching and nodal assays; nitrate assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARR15 is a top CK-induced transcript in buds. 2. Together with ARR3/4/5/6/7 it is required for CK-induced bud activation. 3. The clade remains unnecessary for release after decapitation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Clade disruption compromises branching under favorable conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for architecture modulation, with caution due to complex CK signaling effects.
